name: HMAC
creator: Mihir Bellare, Ran Canetti, Hugo Krawczyk
breakability: No practical attack when using SHA-256 or stronger.
modern_use: API authentication, JWT signatures, TLS record authentication, cookie validation
description: HMAC (Hash-based Message Authentication Code) uses a secret key combined with a hash function to produce a MAC. Unlike a plain hash, it requires the secret key to verify, protecting against both tampering and forgery. Formula: HMAC(K, m) = H((K XOR opad) || H((K XOR ipad) || m))
key_sizes: Any length (recommended: same size as hash output)
notes: Always use constant-time comparison when verifying HMACs to prevent timing attacks.
did_you_know: HMAC-SHA256 is used as the signing algorithm in AWS Signature Version 4, authenticating every API request to AWS services.
